---
title: "Author et al. (Year) — Short Title"
description: "One-sentence takeaway from the paper"
date: 2026-01-01
date-modified: last-modified
categories: [paper notes, NLP, radiology]
---

::: {.note-meta}
🌱 Seedling · Last tended April 2026
:::

## Citation

> Author A, Author B, Author C, et al. **Full paper title.** *Journal Name*. Year;Volume(Issue):Pages. [DOI](https://doi.org/xxx)

## What they did

2–3 sentences. Study design, dataset, main method.

## Key findings

The results that matter, in your own words. Not a list of every table — just what changes how you think about your work.

## Methods worth noting

Technical details you want to remember or adapt. Quote sparingly; paraphrase and annotate.

## Limitations

What they acknowledge and what they don't. Where does their approach break down?

## What this means for my work

The personal layer. How does this paper connect to your projects, change your approach, or open a new question?

## Related

- [Related concept](/notes/concepts/related.qmd)
- [Related project](/projects/project-name/index.qmd)
- [Another paper that contradicts or extends this](/notes/papers/other-author-year.qmd)

::: {.backlinks}
### Linked from
- [Page that links here](/path/to/page.qmd)
:::
